commandsRun: cd /Users/feuvan/src/abyssfire && npm run test -- src/__tests__/QuestNPCIndicators.test.ts src/__tests__/QuestTrackerHUD.test.ts src/__tests__/QuestUXIntegration.test.ts src/__tests__/cross-area-integration.test.ts

assertions.reason: Quest NPC indicator unit tests and real-data integration cover yellow '!' for available quests and yellow '?' for ready-to-turn-in quests.

details: QuestNPCIndicators.test.ts verifies yellow '!' for available quests and yellow '?' for completed quests.
QuestUXIntegration.test.ts real-data elder lifecycle confirms quest_elder starts with '!'.

assertions.reason: State-transition tests confirm indicators update from available to active to completed/turn-in states after quest acceptance and objective completion.

details: QuestNPCIndicators state transition coverage asserts ! -> dim ? after accept and dim ? -> yellow ? after completion.
QuestUXIntegration full lifecycle repeats the same transitions in integration flow.

assertions.reason: Mobile-readiness tests validate DPR-scaled quest UI controls meet or exceed the 44px minimum tap target guidance.

details: Tests assert px(22) >= 44 for indicator/navigation/tracker title targets, px(32) >= 44 for action buttons, and px(24) >= 44 for lore button height.

assertions.reason: Command-only code inspection and tests show quest tracker/card logic is exposed via explicit buttons and computed state, with no scroll-wheel dependency in the validated surfaces.

details: Quest tracker and NPC indicator modules are pure state builders with no mouse-wheel or scroll event handling.
QuestUXIntegration validates navigation through card actions and tracker state transitions without any scroll-only access path.

assertions.reason: End-to-end quest lifecycle integration test covers discovery, acceptance, tracking, progress updates, completion, turn-in, and post-turn-in cleanup.

details: The 'complete lifecycle' test verifies indicator !, accept card, toast, tracker progress updates, completed tracker state, turn-in card, reward data, and indicator removal.

assertions.reason: Save/load integration tests preserve serialized quest progress, tracker signatures, card actions, and NPC indicator states across restore.

details: Save/load tests cover active quests, multi-quest prerequisite chains, and completed ready-to-turn-in state after JSON round-trip.

assertions.reason: The full repository test suite passed with zero failures.

evidence.result: 45 test files passed; 2953 tests passed; 0 failures

assertions.reason: Both TypeScript typecheck and production build completed successfully.

result: vite build succeeded; only existing chunk-size warning reported

assertions.reason: Cross-area and quest integration tests jointly confirm quest UI state works alongside broader system persistence and integration behavior.

details: QuestUXIntegration is annotated to fulfill VAL-CROSS-001 and validates card/tracker/indicator cooperation.
cross-area-integration.test.ts validates save/load round-trip and broader system interactions without quest regressions.

assertions.reason: Zone-transition integration coverage verifies tracker signatures and visible quest entries persist when the same quest data is carried across areas.

details: The zone transition tracker persistence test confirms two quests remain visible and tracker signatures match before/after simulated zone change.

assertions.reason: Full regression signals are green: cross-area integration tests passed and the entire suite passed with no failures.

details: cross-area-integration.test.ts includes regression coverage for inventory, equipment, combat, companions, achievements, gem socketing, and save/migration flows.
Full-suite pass indicates no detected regressions in existing features under command-only validation.

frictions.description: The mission referenced .factory/library/user-testing.md, but that file was absent at the provided path.
frictions.resolution: Used mission AGENTS.md testing guidance plus the explicit parent-task constraints for command-only validation.

frictions.description: Several assigned assertions specify screenshot/console evidence, but task constraints explicitly forbade browser automation and required command-only validation.
frictions.resolution: Mapped those assertions to unit/integration tests, source inspection, typecheck, and build evidence only.

summary: Validated 11 assigned assertions by command-only evidence. Focused quest/cross integration tests, full test suite, typecheck, and production build all passed; no blockers encountered.
